rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 7, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF 345 CALIFORNIA L.P.'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is continued to October 15, 2021 for the moving party to comply with SFLR 2.7B. Courtesy copy of reply to be delivered no later than October 9, 2021 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEWIN PROPERTIES, INC. VS. LORENA HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Lewin Properties, Inc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Demand For Production Of Documents, Set One, Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 7, 2021 line 7. PLAINTIFF LEWIN PROPERTIES, INC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANTIONS is GRANTED. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that an appeal from a denying of a procedurally improper anti-slapp filed in a limited jurisdiction case stays discovery. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES REID VS. JOHN H. MARBLE JR. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 7, 2021 line 8. PLAINTIFF JAMES REID'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden to show that there are no triable issues of fact and that Plaintiff is entitled to adjudication as a matter of law. Triable issue of fact exists whether Defendant is in possession pursuant to the sublease or per a direct agreement with the landlord. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that the validity of the agreement between the Defendant and the landlord can be determined in this unlawful detainer action and in the absence of the landlord. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 7, 2021 line 9. DEFENDANT ROBERT SMALL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clarify whether TPA 2019 is applicable.: Portions of UD-101 suggest applicability of TPA 2019 and the date of lease inception suggests that TPA 2019 is not applicable.
